zh.Kyburg, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germany: 德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg於1227年出生在瑞士阿高勞芬伯格時，他的父親Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg29歲，母親Gertraud von Regensberg26歲。他於1272年與Anna Albrecht在瑞士阿高勞芬伯格結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1284年6月2日在瑞士爾利吉包格享年57歲。

zh-hans.Kyburg, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germany: 德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg于1227年出生在瑞士阿高勞芬伯格时，他的父亲Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg29岁，母亲Gertraud von Regensberg26岁。他于1272年与Anna Albrecht在瑞士阿高勞芬伯格结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1284年6月2日在瑞士爾利吉包格享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Runte  .

variants.name: Runte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erhard.

German: in the northwest of Germany most probably a topographic name from a field name based on run ‘mud’, which gave rise to several placenames in the Rhine-Ruhr area, for example Runten, Runtenberg, Rünthe near Hamm, Westphalia.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Kyburg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut.
Swiss German: probably a variant of the personal name Tiburtius.

nameMeanings.name: Eberhard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from eber ‘boar’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’.

variants.name: Evert
meanings: form of   Eberhard .

en.lifeSummary: When Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg was born in 1227, in Laufenburg, Aargau, Switzerland, his father, Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg, was 29 and his mother, Gertraud von Regensberg, was 26. He married Anna Albrecht in 1272, in Laufenburg, Aargau, Switzerland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 2 June 1284, in Kyburg, Zürich, Switzerland, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg est né en 1227, à Laufenburg, Argovie, Suisse, son père, Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Gertraud von Regensberg, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Anna Albrecht en 1272, à Laufenburg, Argovie, Suisse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 2 juin 1284, à Kyburg, Zurich, Suisse, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg 1227 in Laufenburg, Aargau, Schweiz geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg 29 und seine Mutter Gertraud von Regensberg 26. Er hat 1272 in Laufenburg, Aargau, Schweiz Anna Albrecht geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 2. Juni 1284 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Kyburg, Zürich, Schweiz.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg è nato il nel 1227 nel Laufenburg, Argovia, Svizzera, il suo padre, Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Gertraud von Regensberg, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Anna Albrecht nel 1272, nel Laufenburg, Argovia, Svizzera. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 2 giugno 1284, nel Kyburg, Zurigo, Svizzera, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburgが1227年にスイスアールガウラウフエンブルグで生まれた時，父Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburgは29歳，母Gertraud von Regensbergは26歳であった。1272年にスイスアールガウラウフエンブルグでAnna Albrechtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1284年6月2日にスイスズuリチクイブルグで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg(이)가 1227년 스위스 아아르가 로펜버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Gertraud von Regensberg(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1272년 스위스 아아르가 로펜버그에서 Anna Albrecht(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1284년 6월 2일 스위스 즈u리치 키버그에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg nasceu em 1227, em Laufenburg, Aargau, Suiça, seu pai, Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Gertraud von Regensberg, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Anna Albrecht em 1272, em Laufenburg, Aargau, Suiça. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 2 de junho de 1284, em Kyburg, Zürich, Suiça, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg родился в 1227 г. в местности лауфенбург, ааргау, Швейцария, его отцу по имени Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Gertraud von Regensberg, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Albrecht в 1272 г. в местности лауфенбург, ааргау, Швейцария. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 2 июня 1284 г. в местности кибург, зuрич, Швейцария в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eberhard I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg und von Kyburg nació en 1227, en Laufenburg, Aargau, Suiza, su papá, Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Gertraud von Regensberg, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Anna Albrecht en 1272, en Laufenburg, Aargau, Suiza. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 2 de junio de 1284, en Kyburg, Zürich, Suiza, a la edad de 57 años.
